methodsAttempted.query: site:gju.edu.jo ("artificial intelligence" OR "AI" OR "ChatGPT" OR "generative AI" OR "Academic Integrity" OR "privacy policy" OR "data protection") "German Jordanian University"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned the official Regulations and guidelines page, several official AI news/event items, and academic program pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:gju.edu.jo "Principles for the Use of Artificial Intelligence Tools" "German Jordanian University"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search confirmed the official Regulations and guidelines page as the public discovery route for the AI principles PDF.

methodsAttempted.query: robots.txt and direct HTTP fetch for GJU regulations page and AI principles PDF
methodsAttempted.notes: Direct local HTTP fetches returned status 200 for the HTML page and PDF. robots.txt allowed these paths and declared Crawl-delay: 10.

artifacts.summary: Discovery found an official GJU Regulations and guidelines page linking to a substantive official AI principles PDF. Event/news sources were documented and rejected in favor of the formal PDF.

targets.sourceTitle: Principles for the Use of Artificial Intelligence Tools at the German Jordanian University

Do not infer policy requirements from official event/news pages or academic program pages.

artifacts.claimText: German Jordanian University's AI principles apply to students, researchers, faculty members, and employees, each according to their role.

artifacts.claimText: GJU's AI principles require disclosure of AI tool use in academic and research tasks and say sensitive data should not be entered into AI tools without consent from the relevant authorities and individuals concerned.

citation.citationTitle: Principles for the Use of Artificial Intelligence Tools at the German Jordanian University
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: GJU's AI principles permit students to use AI tools for learning-supportive activities such as preliminary research, analysis, idea generation, writing improvement, and summarizing academic sources, provided that the use is disclosed; the principles prohibit using AI to fully prepare projects or theses without the student's actual contribution.

citation.citationTitle: Principles for the Use of Artificial Intelligence Tools at the German Jordanian University
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: GJU's AI principles permit AI tools for research support such as data analysis and research model development, require researchers to disclose AI tool use, and prohibit using AI tools to produce complete research papers, fabricate data, generate non-original literature reviews, or create content not based on genuine research effort.

citation.citationTitle: Principles for the Use of Artificial Intelligence Tools at the German Jordanian University
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: GJU's AI principles say misuse of AI tools, including plagiarism, cheating, and violations of academic integrity principles, will be handled under the disciplinary procedures in relevant university regulations and instructions.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Principles for the Use of Artificial Intelligence Tools at the German Jordanian University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Transparency and Accountability: Disclose the use of any artificial intelligence tools in academic and research tasks and scholarly outputs, specifying the tool's name, how it was used, where it was applied in the scholarly work, and its impact on the results.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Article (5), Principles of Use, Transparency and Accountability

artifacts.sourceTitle: Principles for the Use of Artificial Intelligence Tools at the German Jordanian University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Data Protection and Privacy: Comply with laws, regulations, and guidelines related to personal data protection, including the Jordanian Personal Data Protection Law and University policies. Avoid inputting sensitive data into AI tools without obtaining consent from the relevant authorities and individuals concerned.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Article (5), Principles of Use, Data Protection and Privacy

artifacts.sourceTitle: Principles for the Use of Artificial Intelligence Tools at the German Jordanian University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Limits of Student Use: Students are permitted to use AI tools in learning-supportive activities such as preliminary research, analysis, idea generation, writing improvement, and summarizing academic sources, provided that the usage is disclosed. However, AI use is prohibited for fully preparing projects or theses without the student's actual contribution
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Article (6), Use of Artificial Intelligence in Education, Limits of Student Use

artifacts.title: German Jordanian University AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Staged crawl for German Jordanian University verified the official GJU Regulations and guidelines page and the official AI principles PDF. The PDF supports candidate claims on scope, disclosure and sensitive data handling, student educational use limits, research use limits, and accountability for AI misuse. Official AI event/news pages were rejected as lower-specificity sources and not used for claims.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Candidate claims are staged artifacts only and are not canonical public claims until separately reviewed and promoted.
Event/news pages and academic program pages discovered during search were not used as policy evidence.
The official AI principles PDF is bilingual; this staged run uses the English source text from the PDF as the quoted original evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
